content.problemStatement:
Chef has a rectangular field of dimensions N×MN \times MN×M which contains grass all over it. (0,0)(0,0)(0,0) is made the bottom left corner of the field. (N,M)(N, M)(N,M) is made the top right corner of the field.

Aliens aim to destroy the field by sending a cow on the field which will eat all the grass.

Chef, in order to protect the field, constructs KKK rectangular electric fences.

An electric fence is represented by x1 y1 x2 y2x_1\space  y_1\space x_2\space y_2x1​ y1​ x2​ y2​. This denotes an electric fence along the perimeter of the rectangle with bottom left corner as (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1​,y1​) and top right corner as (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2​,y2​). The fences can touch or intersect with each other.

The aliens will send the cow to a point PPP on the field. From that point PPP, the cow will graze all the grass it can reach. The cow is intelligent enough to know that electric fence can kill it. Therefore, it will never touch or cross an electric fence. Also, the cow will never leave the field.

The aliens can't see the fences from above and thus will send the cow to any point (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) (not necessarily an integer point) selected uniformly at random on the field. (0≤x≤N,0≤y≤M)(0 \le x \le N, 0 \le y \le M)(0≤x≤N,0≤y≤M)

Chef asks you to calculate the expected area of grass lost due to this alien invasion. Since the Chef doesn't like floating point numbers, he wants you to tell the value of P×Q−1 mod 109+7P \times Q^{-1} \space mod \space 10^9+7P×Q−1 mod 109+7. Here PQ=\frac{P}{Q} = QP​= expected grass area destroyed with gcd(P,Q)=1gcd(P,Q)=1gcd(P,Q)=1. Also, Q−1Q^{-1}Q−1 is the modular multiplicative inverse of QQQ with respect to 109+710^9+7109+7.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains three integers N,M,KN, M, KN,M,K denoting the dimensions of the field and the number of rectangular electric fences. Then KKK lines follow.
Each line contains 444 integers x1 y1 x2 y2x_1\space  y_1\space x_2\space y_2x1​ y1​ x2​ y2​ denoting the electric fence.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line the answer to the problem.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of test cases T. Each test case consists of three integers N, M, and K, representing the dimensions of the field and the number of rectangular electric fences. Then, K lines follow, each containing four integers x1, y1, x2, and y2, denoting the electric fence.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the expected area of grass lost due to the alien invasion, calculated as P * Q^-1 mod 10^9 + 7, where P/Q is the expected grass area destroyed with gcd(P, Q) = 1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Testcase 1: The Chef divides the whole field into 4 equal parts. Therefore 1/4th of the field will be grazed by the cow wherever the cow lands. Expected area lost = 10 * 10 / 4 = 25.
Testcase 2: The Chef, using 2 fences, divides the whole field into 3 equal parts. Therefore 1/3rd of the field will be grazed by the cow wherever the cow lands. Expected area lost = 9 * 9 / 3 = 27.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to calculate the expected area of grass lost due to the alien invasion. We can do this by dividing the field into regions separated by the electric fences and calculating the area of each region. Then, we can calculate the expected area lost by summing up the areas of the regions that the cow can reach from each point in the field.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * K * N * M) — the time complexity is dominated by the nested loops over the test cases, electric fences, and the field dimensions.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * M) — the space complexity is dominated by the storage of the field dimensions.

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys
MOD = 1000000007

T = int(sys.stdin.readline())
for _ in range(T):
    N, M, K = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())
    area = 0
    for _ in range(K):
        x1, y1, x2, y2 = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())
        # Calculate the area of the region separated by the electric fence
        regionArea = (x2 - x1) * (y2 - y1)
        area += regionArea
    # Calculate the expected area lost
    expectedArea = (N * M - area) // (K + 1)
    print(expectedArea % MOD)
